title: Markdown Review Sidecar Format (MRSF) v1.0
description: Schema for MRSF review sidecar files. See MRSF-v1.0.md for the full specification.

mrsf_version.description: MRSF format version. MUST be a supported major.minor version (e.g., 1.0).

document.description: Relative path to the Markdown document being reviewed.

id.description: Globally unique, opaque, collision-resistant identifier for the comment.

author.description: Creator of the comment. SHOULD follow the convention 'Display Name (identifier)'.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp of comment creation; SHOULD include timezone offset.

text.description: The content of the review comment. MUST be plain text.

resolved.description: Whether the comment has been resolved.

commit.description: Git commit hash associated with the comment. SHOULD be the full (long) SHA.

type.description: Categorization of the comment. Recommended values listed in examples.

reply_to.description: ID of another comment in the same file that this comment replies to.

line.description: Starting line number (1-based) in the target document.

end_line.description: Ending line number (inclusive, 1-based). MUST be >= line.

start_column.description: Starting column index (0-based) within the starting line.

end_column.description: Ending column index. MUST be >= start_column when on the same line.

selected_text.description: Exact text selected by the reviewer. SHOULD NOT be modified by re-anchoring tools. SHOULD NOT exceed 4096 characters.

anchored_text.description: Text currently found at the resolved anchor position. Populated by re-anchoring tools when the document text differs from selected_text. SHOULD be omitted when identical to selected_text.

selected_text_hash.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 hash of selected_text. Immutable after creation; used for fast exact-match detection during re-anchoring, integrity verification, and staleness checks. SHOULD NOT be modified by re-anchoring tools unless selected_text is also replaced (opt-in behaviour), in which case it MUST be recomputed.
